description: Phase 1 — Core value features: data richness, UX power, and first whimsy hook

userStories.title: OpenLibrary ISBN auto-fill in Add Book form
userStories.description: As a user, I want to type an ISBN and have the book's title, author, publisher, and description auto-populated from OpenLibrary, so I don't have to type everything manually.
acceptanceCriteria: Add an 'Lookup' button next to the ISBN field in the Add Book form
Clicking the button calls GET /api/books/openlibrary/{isbn} with the current ISBN value
On success, title, author first/last name, and description fields are populated from the response
Button shows a loading state while the request is in flight
If the ISBN is not found, show a non-blocking inline error message near the ISBN field
If the ISBN field is empty, the button is disabled
All existing form validation and submission behaviour is unchanged
Frontend typechecks pass: cd ClientApp && npm run build
Backend builds: dotnet build
userStories.notes: The backend endpoint GET /api/books/openlibrary/{isbn} already exists in BooksController.cs and OpenLibraryService.cs. The OpenLibraryBook model returns Title, Subtitle, Authors (as key refs), PublishDate, NumberOfPages, Publishers. Authors come back as key refs only (e.g. '/authors/OL1A') — use the first publisher from Publishers[] and the PublishDate for date. For author name, the OpenLibrary ISBN endpoint does not return full names directly; populate what's available and leave name fields blank if not resolvable. Wire the existing booksApi.ts or add a new lookup function.

userStories.title: Reading Status field on books
userStories.description: As a reader, I want to mark each book as 'Want to Read', 'Reading', or 'Read', so I can track my reading progress.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a ReadingStatus enum to the Book entity: WantToRead, Reading, Read
Add readingStatus column to the database via a new EF Core migration
Default value is WantToRead
Expose readingStatus in BookReadDto and BookCreateDto
Add a status dropdown/select to both the Add Book form and the Edit Book form
Display the reading status badge on the book detail page and in the book list
Status badge uses distinct colours: WantToRead = slate, Reading = amber, Read = green
Frontend typechecks pass: cd ClientApp && npm run build
Backend builds: dotnet build
userStories.notes: Add the enum in Models/Entities/Book.cs. Create a migration with: dotnet ef migrations add AddReadingStatus. Update BookReadDto.cs and BookCreateDto.cs. Update Mapster mappings if needed (MapsterConfig.cs). Update BooksPage reducer and BookDetailPage reducer to handle the new field. The BookForm component should get a new select field — see existing Field subcomponent pattern in BookForm.tsx.

userStories.title: Translator and Publisher fields
userStories.description: As a reader of translated literature, I want to record the translator and publisher of a book, so I can credit translators as co-authors and track small-press publishers.
acceptanceCriteria: Add translator (string, max 200, optional) and publisher (string, max 200, optional) columns to the Book entity and database via a new EF Core migration
Expose translator and publisher in BookReadDto and BookCreateDto
Add Translator and Publisher input fields to both the Add Book form and the Edit Book form
Display translator (if present) on the book detail page, labelled 'Translated by'
Display publisher (if present) on the book detail page
Frontend typechecks pass: cd ClientApp && npm run build
Backend builds: dotnet build
userStories.notes: Similar pattern to any other Book scalar field addition. Migration: dotnet ef migrations add AddTranslatorAndPublisher. Update BookForm.tsx to add two new text fields using the existing Field component pattern. Update the detail page display section. Update the TypeScript Book type in models/book.ts.

userStories.title: Search and filter in the book list
userStories.description: As a user with a growing collection, I want to search by title or author and filter by reading status, so I can quickly find the book I'm looking for.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a text search input above the book list on BooksPage
Search filters the displayed list client-side by title and author name (case-insensitive, partial match)
Add a reading status filter dropdown (All / Want to Read / Reading / Read) next to the search input
Search and filter work together (both active simultaneously)
When no results match, show a friendly empty state message
Search input is debounced (300ms) to avoid excessive re-renders
Filter state is local to the component — no URL persistence needed for now
Frontend typechecks pass: cd ClientApp && npm run build
userStories.notes: This is entirely a frontend change. The full book list is already loaded into state in BooksPage.tsx — just add a derived filtered list using useMemo. Follow the existing Elm-style reducer pattern: add SearchQueryChanged and StatusFilterChanged Msg variants. No backend changes needed. Reading status filter depends on US-002 being completed first.

userStories.title: 'Currently Reading' bookmark in the SNES bookshelf
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a small bookmark sticking out of the spine of the book I'm currently reading in the bookshelf room, so the shelf feels alive and personal.
acceptanceCriteria: Books with readingStatus = 'Reading' display a small bookmark tab sticking up above their spine in the SNES bookshelf room
The bookmark is a bright red/crimson colour, 3px wide, 8px tall, positioned at the top of the spine
If multiple books have status Reading, all of them show a bookmark
The bookmark does not interfere with hover tooltips or click navigation
The bookmark is rendered using Kaplay primitives (rect or similar), consistent with the existing pixel-art aesthetic
Frontend typechecks pass: cd ClientApp && npm run build
userStories.notes: All changes are in BookshelfRoom.tsx. After the spine rect is drawn for each book, check if book.readingStatus === 'Reading' and draw an additional rect above the spine top. The Book type from the API will now include readingStatus after US-002. Use the existing Kaplay add() / rect() pattern already in the file. Keep it pixel-perfect: 3px wide, 8-10px tall, offset slightly from centre of the spine.
